1: Why art thou so Why art thou so far from helping me, and from and from the words of my roaring?
2: And in the night season, and am not silent.
3: O thou O thou that inhabitest the praises of Israel.
4: They trusted, and thou didst deliver them.
5: They trusted in thee, and were not confounded.
6: A reproach of men, and despised of the people.
7: They shoot out the lip, they shake the head, saying saying ,
8: Let him deliver him, seeing he delighted in him.
9: Thou didst make me hope when I was when I was upon my mother's breasts.
10: Thou art art my God from my mother's belly.
11: For there is there is none to help.
12: Strong bulls bulls of Bashan have beset me round.
13: As As a ravening and a roaring lion.
14: My heart is like wax; It is melted in the midst of my bowels.
15: And thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
16: They pierced my hands and my feet.
17: They look and and stare upon me.
18: And cast lots upon my vesture.
19: O my strength, haste thee to help me.
20: My darling from the power of the dog.
21: For thou hast heard me from the horns of the unicorns.
22: In the midst of the congregation will I praise thee.
23: All ye the seed of Jacob, glorify him; And fear him, all ye the seed of Israel.
24: Neither hath he hid his face from him; But when he cried unto him, he heard.
25: I will pay my vows before them that fear him.
26: They shall praise the LORD LORD that seek him: Your heart shall live for ever.
27: And all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.
28: And he is is the governor among the nations.
29: And none can keep alive his own soul.
30: It shall be accounted to the LORD LORD for a generation.
31: Unto a people that shall be born, that he hath done this this .
